content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Enrollment Components & Troubleshooting Tips | Student Services

  - url: https://studentservices.stanford.edu/my-academics/enroll-classes/enrollment-troubleshooting

  - source: Stanford Student Services

  - domain: studentservices.stanford.edu


  ## Content


  # Enrollment Components & Troubleshooting Tips


  Stanford Student Services lists several different reasons a student may be unable
  to enroll through Navigate Enrollment. The cause of the problem determines the appropriate
  next step.


  ## Why enrollment may fail


  A student who cannot enroll should first review the message shown in Navigate Enrollment.
  Common causes include:


  1. **Enrollment hold.** A red banner may indicate a hold. The student should open
  Axess and review the Holds, To Do''s and Notifications section for the hold details
  and required action.


  2. **Enrollment has not opened for the student.** Enrollment opens according to
  Stanford''s academic calendar and assigned enrollment groups. The student should
  confirm the relevant quarter and enrollment start time.


  3. **The class is full.** A closed class or a class that has reached its capacity
  cannot accept additional enrollment. A waitlist may be available.


  4. **Incorrect academic term.** Navigate Enrollment can display more than one active
  quarter, so the student should verify that the correct term was selected.


  When these checks do not explain the problem and the student is still unable to
  enroll, Student Services directs the student to submit a help ticket to the Student
  Services Center. For troubleshooting, the Center recommends attaching screenshots
  taken immediately before and after the enrollment request, along with the browser
  and computer platform used.


  ## Permission numbers


  Some classes limit enrollment by requiring a permission number. A permission number
  represents individual approval for a specific class in a particular quarter. It
  is valid for one use.


  If a student enrolls with a permission number, drops the class, and later wants
  to enroll again, a new number is required. For combined-section classes, the number
  must apply to the particular section the student is trying to join.


  A class may require a permission number because the instructor or department is
  controlling admission to the class. If a permission number does not work or a new
  one is required, the student should contact the instructor or department for further
  support.


  ## Requisites and overrides


  Courses may use several forms of requisites:


  - A **prerequisite** is a course or condition that must be completed before enrollment.

  - A **corequisite** must be taken at the same time as the requested course.

  - An **antirequisite** prevents enrollment when the student has completed another
  course with overlapping content.


  Course descriptions in Navigate Classes normally identify these requirements.


  External test credit, including AP or IB credit, may sometimes satisfy a requisite
  even when the enrollment system does not recognize it automatically. In that situation,
  the student should contact the department offering the course to request a permission
  number.


  For transfer work, only course-to-course equivalency credit can be used for enrollment
  requisites. A student who believes qualifying transfer credit should satisfy a requisite
  should contact the department offering the course to request a permission number.


  ## Waitlist and enrollment-access issues


  Waitlist enrollment can also fail because no seat has opened, an enrollment hold
  exists, the requested class conflicts with the student''s current schedule, or enrollment
  would exceed the maximum unit limit. These conditions are separate from a course
  permission requirement.


  A student with an enrollment hold should review the hold in Axess and may contact
  the Student Services Center for information about the hold. A time conflict or excess-unit
  condition must be corrected before the waitlist can enroll the student.


  The correct destination therefore depends on the active problem: Student Services
  supports unresolved registration-access and system issues, while course-specific
  permission numbers and requisite overrides are handled through the instructor or
  department controlling the course.

  '

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Enroll in a Class | Student Services
\
  - url: https://studentservices.stanford.edu/my-academics/enroll-classes/how-do-i-enroll/enroll-class
\
  - source: Stanford Student Services
- domain: studentservices.stanford.edu

\
  ## Content

# Enroll in a Class

Stanford students use Navigate Enrollment to\
  \ add an individual class. The enrollment workflow includes reviewing the class\
  \ offering, responding to class-specific prompts, submitting the request, and checking\
  \ the resulting confirmation message.

## Step-by-step process

1. **Open Navigate\
  \ Enrollment.** Access Navigate Enrollment from the Axess login page.

2. **Search\
  \ for the class.** Select Class Search, confirm the academic term, and search by\
  \ subject, course number, or keyword. Search results can be filtered by instructor,\
  \ units, meeting days, and other attributes.

3. **Review class details.** Open\
  \ the class details and review meeting times, requisites, grading-basis options,\
  \ and available sections. Select Enroll when the intended offering is confirmed.
\
  
4. **Respond to enrollment prompts.** Depending on the class setup, the system\
  \ may ask for the grading basis, number of units, an associated lab or discussion\
  \ section, waitlist preferences, a class to drop if enrolled, a career selection,\
  \ or a permission number.

5. **Review and submit.** Check the enrollment summary\
  \ and submit the request.

6. **Read the confirmation.** Navigate Enrollment reports\
  \ whether the request succeeded, placed the student on a waitlist, or failed. If\
  \ enrollment failed, the student should review the displayed message for details.
\
  
## Class-specific prompts

Associated sections should be selected carefully.\
  \ If a lab or discussion section is required and not chosen during enrollment, changing\
  \ the selection may require dropping and re-enrolling.

A permission-number field\
  \ appears only when the class setup requires instructor or department approval,\
  \ such as a course with an application process or controlled enrollment. The field\
  \ is part of the enrollment transaction; it does not itself determine whether the\
  \ student is eligible for approval.

Waitlist prompts may allow the student to\
  \ request a place on the waitlist when the class is full. The “Drop if Enrolled”\
  \ option can identify an existing class to drop automatically if the waitlist later\
  \ enrolls the student.

## Reviewing errors

The final confirmation distinguishes\
  \ a successful enrollment, a waitlist result, and a failed request. The exact message\
  \ matters because different failures have different causes.

Before enrolling,\
  \ students should review course requisites. Navigate Enrollment may prevent enrollment\
  \ when a required prerequisite, corequisite, or other requisite is not satisfied.\
  \ Students should also confirm that they selected the correct term and class section.
\
  
Time conflicts generally do not prevent ordinary enrollment, but they can prevent\
  \ enrollment from a waitlist. Some conflict situations may require a course permission\
  \ number.

This page describes how to perform and review an enrollment transaction.\
  \ It does not treat every failed request as the same type of problem; the confirmation\
  \ message and the class setup provide the information needed for further troubleshooting.
"

args.query: Stanford CS229 course enrollment prerequisite external test credit permission number

content.content:
The problem is **not a hold, closed class, waitlist, or enrollment-window issue**. Your CS229 enrollment attempt was rejected because:

> **“The enrollment system could not verify the required prerequisite.”**  
> Diagnostic: `REQUISITE_NOT_RECOGNIZED`  
> Attempt ID: `ENR-ATT-20481`

Your record shows the prerequisite is satisfied through **external test credit**, but that credit is not being recognized automatically by the enrollment system.

### Who can resolve it

Contact the **Stanford Computer Science department or the CS229 instructor/course staff**. They handle course-specific prerequisite overrides and permission numbers. Stanford’s enrollment guidance specifically says that when external test credit satisfies a prerequisite but is not recognized automatically, students should contact the department offering the course to request a **permission number**.

### What to do next

1. Contact the CS229 instructor or CS department course staff promptly.
2. Explain that:
   - CS229 section 01 is open.
   - Your prerequisite is satisfied by external test credit.
   - Navigate Enrollment rejected your request with `REQUISITE_NOT_RECOGNIZED`.
   - Your enrollment attempt ID is `ENR-ATT-20481`.
3. Ask them to verify the external credit and provide a **permission number or prerequisite override**.
4. Enter the permission number in Navigate Enrollment and resubmit your CS229 enrollment request.
5. If the department confirms your eligibility but the system still rejects the request, submit a ticket to the **Stanford Student Services Center**, attaching screenshots of the error and the attempt details. Stanford recommends screenshots from immediately before and after submitting the enrollment request.

Relevant Stanford guidance: [Enrollment Components & Troubleshooting Tips](https://studentservices.stanford.edu/my-academics/enroll-classes/enrollment-troubleshooting).

### Who can resolve it

Stanford’s enrollment guidance specifically says that when external test credit satisfies a prerequisite but is not recognized automatically, students should contact the department offering the course to request a **permission number**.

### What to do next

1. Contact the CS229 instructor or CS department course staff promptly.
2. Explain that:
   - CS229 section 01 is open.

4. Enter the permission number in Navigate Enrollment and resubmit your CS229 enrollment request.
5. If the department confirms your eligibility but the system still rejects the request, submit a ticket to the **Stanford Student Services Center**, attaching screenshots of the error and the attempt details. Stanford recommends screenshots from immediately before and after submitting the enrollment request.

^
(?!.*(?:do\s+not|don't|should\s+not|shouldn't|avoid|instead\s+of)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:contact(?!\s+(?:email|information|details|page|website))|contacting|ask(?:ing)?|request(?:ing)?|use|follow|go\s+through|work\s+with|reach\s+out\s+to)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:department\s+offering(?:\s+the)?\s+course|offering\s+department|computer\s+science(?:\s+department)?|cs\s+department|academic\s+department|academic\s+unit|course\s+instructor|instructor|course\s+staff|instructional\s+staff)
)
(?!.*(?:department\s+offering(?:\s+the)?\s+course|offering\s+department|computer\s+science(?:\s+department)?|cs\s+department|academic\s+department|academic\s+unit|course\s+instructor|instructor|course\s+staff|instructional\s+staff)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:cannot|can't|does\s+not|doesn't|should\s+not|shouldn't|is\s+not|isn't|are\s+not|aren't)
    [^.!?\n]{0,80}
    (?:help|handle|review|verify|issue|grant|approve|provide|resolve|apply)
)
(?!.*(?:department\s+offering(?:\s+the)?\s+course|offering\s+department|computer\s+science(?:\s+department)?|cs\s+department|academic\s+department|academic\s+unit|course\s+instructor|instructor|course\s+staff|instructional\s+staff)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:is\s+not|isn't|not)\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:right|correct|responsible|appropriate)\s+(?:office|destination|contact|path|route|party)
)
(?!.*(?:first|initially|primary|main|only)
    [^.!?\n]{0,50}
    (?:contact|ask|use|go\s+through|reach\s+out\s+to)
    [^.!?\n]{0,80}
    (?:registrar|student\s+services(?:\s+center)?)
)
(?!.*(?:contact|ask|use|go\s+through|reach\s+out\s+to)\s+(?:the\s+)?
    (?:registrar|student\s+services(?:\s+center)?)
    \s*(?:,?\s*\b(?:or|and|either|both)\b\s*|\s+as\s+well\s+as\s+)
    (?:the\s+)?(?:department|instructor|academic\s+unit|course\s+staff|computer\s+science)
)
(?!.*(?:contact|ask|use|go\s+through|reach\s+out\s+to)\s+(?:the\s+)?
    (?:department|instructor|academic\s+unit|course\s+staff|computer\s+science)
    \s*(?:,?\s*\b(?:or|and|either|both)\b\s*|\s+as\s+well\s+as\s+)
    (?:the\s+)?(?:registrar|student\s+services(?:\s+center)?)
)
(?!.*(?:request|obtain|get|seek)
    [^.!?\n]{0,80}
    (?:permission[\s_-]*(?:number|code)|prerequisite[\s_-]*override|requisite[\s_-]*override)
    [^.!?\n]{0,80}
    (?:from|through|via)\s+(?:the\s+)?
    (?:registrar|student\s+services(?:\s+center)?)
)
(?!.*(?:registrar|student\s+services(?:\s+center)?)
    [^.!?\n]{0,50}
    (?:(?:can|will|should|must)\s+)?(?:issue|grant|approve|provide|review)
    \s+(?:a\s+|the\s+|your\s+|this\s+|course\s+)?
    (?:permission|override|prerequisite|requisite)
)
(?=.*(?:
    (?:external|outside)\s+(?:(?:test|exam)\s+)?(?:credit|score|coursework)
  | ap(?:\s*/\s*|\s+or\s+|\s+and\s+)?ib\s+credit
  | ap\s+credit
  | ib\s+credit
  | transfer\s+(?:credit|coursework)
  | exam\s+(?:credit|score)
  | test\s+credit
))
(?=.*(?:
    requisite[\s_-]*not[\s_-]*recognized
  | prerequisite[\s_-]*not[\s_-]*recognized
  | not\s+(?:automatically\s+)?recognized
  | could\s+not\s+(?:verify|recognize)
  | couldn't\s+(?:verify|recognize)
  | failed\s+to\s+(?:recognize|verify)
  | unable\s+to\s+(?:recognize|verify)
  | system[^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:does\s+not|doesn't|did\s+not|didn't|has\s+not|hasn't|could\s+not|couldn't|failed\s+to)
    [^.!?\n]{0,60}(?:recognize|verify|accept|apply)
))
(?=.*(?:
    permission[\s_-]*(?:number|code)
  | prerequisite[\s_-]*override
  | requisite[\s_-]*override
  | course[\s_-]*permission
  | instructor[\s_-]*consent
  | department[\s_-]*consent
))
(?=.*(?:
    (?:contact|ask|request|use|follow|go\s+through|work\s+with|reach\s+out\s+to|handled\s+by|reviewed\s+by)
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:department\s+offering(?:\s+the)?\s+course|offering\s+department|computer\s+science(?:\s+department)?|cs\s+department|academic\s+department|academic\s+unit|course\s+instructor|instructor|course\s+staff|instructional\s+staff)
  |
    (?:department\s+offering(?:\s+the)?\s+course|offering\s+department|computer\s+science(?:\s+department)?|cs\s+department|academic\s+department|academic\s+unit|course\s+instructor|instructor|course\s+staff|instructional\s+staff)
    [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    (?:handles?|reviews?|verifies?|can\s+(?:issue|grant|approve|provide)|should\s+(?:issue|review|provide)|is\s+responsible)
))
.*
$
